source: .claude/skills/ir:onboard-agent/scenario-meanings.md → user-blocking-question (full arc: question, then user answers)
notes: Full user-blocking-question arc on one live codex rollout session. Turn 1: codex ends its reply with a single clarifying question (literal `?`), so at the turn's task_complete (turn_done) IsAgentDone() is true AND the last assistant message ends in `?` — the daemon takes the trailing-? text path (session.IsWaitingForUserInput → state_classifier.go decision 2a) and transitions working→waiting instead of working→ready. The user then ANSWERS (turn 2): the session returns to working, codex completes the turn with a plain non-question reply, and the classifier falls to decision 2b (ready). So the observable arc is ready→working→waiting→working→ready, all on the SAME session_id — the waiting episode is visible (no skip-over) and the cell ends in ready, unlike the sibling agent-question-pending which stops at waiting. Codex has NO structured AskUserQuestion tool (matrix prior); this is the adapter-agnostic text classifier. Observable ONLY via the interactive TUI: headless `codex exec` is single-shot and exits at turn end (process_exited race, see basic-turn spec), so the multi-turn arc would be lost. The interactive driver (drive-codex-interactive.sh) keeps the rollout session alive across both turns — the REPL is torn down only at script end — and a trailing sleep after each wait_turn lets each state episode settle. The daemon sees the proc-PID pre-session born ready, then the rollout session reconcile (new session_id) born ready, then working, waiting, working again, then ready, then process_exited at teardown.

text: Pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch
Rollout session reconciles (different session_id), born ready
PID bound to the rollout session
First user prompt → working on the rollout session
Agent's first reply ends in a question — turn ends in waiting, not ready
The waiting episode is visible and persists while the agent is gated on the user — no skip-over to ready before the user answers
User answers the question → session returns to working (waiting→working) on the same session
Agent completes the turn with a plain reply (no trailing question) → ready (working→ready)
Driver tears down the REPL after the recovery — process_exited on the rollout session
